km.Everingham, Yorkshire, England: អេវីeរីnហ្គាម, យ៉ុរកស៊ីរe, ប្រទេសអង់គ្លេស

zh.lifeSummary: 當Edward Purchase de Everingham於1101年出生在英國越可爾艾華靈漢時，他的父親Everingham I27歲，母親Everingham26歲。他至少有1個兒子。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Edward Purchase de Everingham于1101年出生在英国越可爾艾華靈漢时，他的父亲Everingham I27岁，母亲Everingham26岁。他至少有1个儿子。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Everingham
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English (East Yorkshire): habitational name from Everingham (East Yorkshire), named in Old English Yferingaham ‘homestead (hām) of the people (-inga-) of Eofor’.

nameMeanings.name: Edward
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: From an Old English personal name derived from ēad ‘prosperity, riches’ + weard ‘guard’. This has been one of the most successful of all Old English names, in frequent use from before the Conquest to the present day, and even being exported into other European languages. It was the name of three Anglo-Saxon kings and has been borne by eight kings of England since the Norman Conquest. It is also the name of the youngest son of Queen Elizabeth II . The most influential early bearer was King Edward the Confessor ( ?1002–66 ; ruled 1042–66 ). In a troubled period of English history, he contrived to rule fairly and (for a time at any rate) firmly. But in the latter part of his reign he paid more attention to his religion than to his kingdom. He died childless, and his death sparked off conflicting claims to his throne, which were resolved by the victory of William the Conqueror at the Battle of Hastings. His memory was honoured by Normans and English alike, for his fairness and his piety. Edward's mother was Norman; he had spent part of his youth in Normandy; and William claimed to have been nominated by Edward as his successor. Edward was canonized in the 12th century, and came to be venerated throughout Europe as a model of a Christian king.

nameMeanings.name: Purchase
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English (of Norman origin): perhaps an occupational name or nickname from Old French, Middle English purchas(e), purchace, porchas ‘acquisition, gain, profit’, used in a transferred sense, denoting an official responsible for obtaining the supplies required by a monastery, manor, or similar. Alternatively, from the same word with the primary senses ‘search, pursuit, effort’, possibly used for messengers and couriers.

variants.name: Purkiss
meanings: English (Essex): usually a variant of   Purchase  , sometimes of   Perkins  .

historicEvents.title: The Battle of Tichebray
historicEvents.description: King Henry I defeated and captured his brother, Robert of Normandy, at the Battle of Tinchebray in 1106. Robert remained in captivity until his death in 1134.

historicEvents.title: Magna Carta
historicEvents.description: King John of England agreed to a charter of rights called the Magna Carta. The document was originally drafted by the Archbishop of Canterbury to establish peace between King John and a group of rebel barons. The Magna Carta was completed in 1215 and is now considered to be one of the greatest constitutional documents of all time.

en.lifeSummary: When Edward Purchase de Everingham was born in 1101, in Everingham, Yorkshire, England, his father, Everingham I, was 27 and his mother, Everingham, was 26. He had at least 1 son.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Edward Purchase d’Everingham est né en 1101, à Everingham, Everwyck, Angleterre, son père, Everingham I, avait 27 ans et sa mère, Everingham, avait 26 ans. Il a eu au moins 1 fils.

de.lifeSummary: Als Edward Purchase de Everingham 1101 in Everingham, Yorkshire, England geboren wurde, war sein Vater Everingham I 27 und seine Mutter Everingham 26. Er hatte mindestens 1 Sohn.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Edward Purchase de Everingham è nato il nel 1101 nel Everingham, Yorkshire, England, il suo padre, Everingham I, aveva 27 anni e il suo madre, Everingham, aveva 26 anni. Lui avuto almeno 1 figlio.

ja.Everingham, Yorkshire, England: イギリスヨークシュアイアエバーインガム
ja.lifeSummary: Edward Purchase de Everinghamが1101年にイギリスヨークシュアイアエバーインガムで生まれた時，父Everingham Iは27歳，母Everinghamは26歳であった。彼は少なくとも1人の息子をもうけた。

ko.lifeSummary: Edward Purchase de Everingham(이)가 1101년 영국 요르크실르 에버링함에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Everingham I(은)는 27세였고, 그의 어머니 Everingham(은)는 26세였다. 그는 적어도 한 명의 아들을 두었다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Edward Purchase de Everingham nasceu em 1101, em Everingham, Yorkshire, Inglaterra, seu pai, Everingham I, tinha 27 anos e sua mãe, Everingham, tinha 26 anos. Ele teve pelo menos 1 filho.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Edward Purchase de Everingham родился в 1101 г. в местности эвирингхам, еркшир, Англия, его отцу по имени Everingham I было 27 лет, а матери, которую звали Everingham, 26. У него был по крайней мере 1 сын.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Edward Purchase de Everingham nació en 1101, en Everingham, Yorkshire, Inglaterra, su papá, Everingham I, tenía 27 años y su mamá, Everingham, tenía 26 años. Tuvo por lo menos 1 hijo.
